data.title: Legacy Education Inc. Reports Steady Revenue Growth
data.description:
Legacy Education Inc. ( (LGCY) ) has released its Q1 earnings. Here is a breakdown of the information Legacy Education Inc. presented to its investors.

Meet Your ETF AI Analyst

  • Discover how TipRanks' ETF AI Analyst can help you make smarter investment decisions
  • Explore ETFs TipRanks' users love and see what insights the ETF AI Analyst reveals about the ones you follow.

Legacy Education Inc., operating in the post-secondary education sector, offers career-focused programs through its accredited institutions, providing training in fields such as healthcare and business management. The company, headquartered in California, is known for its practical approach to education and diverse program offerings.

In its latest earnings report for the quarter ending September 30, 2025, Legacy Education Inc. reported a net income of $2.19 million, a slight increase from the previous year’s $2.09 million. The company also saw a notable rise in revenue, reaching $19.4 million, up from $14 million in the same quarter last year, driven by increased tuition and related income.

Key financial highlights include a significant increase in operating income, which reached $2.7 million, reflecting efficient cost management and higher enrollment rates. The company’s cash and cash equivalents also saw a modest increase, indicating a stable financial position. Additionally, Legacy Education’s stockholders’ equity rose to $43.67 million, up from $41.04 million at the end of the previous quarter, showcasing robust financial health.

Looking ahead, Legacy Education Inc. remains focused on expanding its program offerings and maintaining compliance with regulatory standards. The management is optimistic about future growth opportunities, supported by strategic initiatives and a strong financial foundation.

data.title: Worksport Ltd. Reports Strong Q3 Growth, Eyes 2026 Profitability
data.description:
Worksport Ltd ( (WKSP) ) has released its Q3 earnings. Here is a breakdown of the information Worksport Ltd presented to its investors.

Meet Your ETF AI Analyst

  • Discover how TipRanks' ETF AI Analyst can help you make smarter investment decisions
  • Explore ETFs TipRanks' users love and see what insights the ETF AI Analyst reveals about the ones you follow.

Worksport Ltd., a U.S.-based company, specializes in advanced manufacturing and distributed clean energy technologies, focusing on consumer and industrial markets.

In its latest earnings report, Worksport Ltd. announced a significant 61% increase in revenue for the third quarter of 2025, achieving a gross margin of 31.3%. The company is optimistic about reaching profitability in 2026, driven by its innovative product lines, including the SOLIS solar-integrated tonneau cover and the COR battery system.

Key financial highlights include year-to-date sales of $11.4 million, more than doubling the previous year’s figures, and a notable improvement in gross margins compared to prior periods. The company has also strengthened its balance sheet by reducing total debt and maintaining flexibility through preserved revolving capacity. Worksport’s product lineup, including the HD3 Heavy-Duty Tonneau and the upcoming SOLIS and COR launches, is expected to contribute significantly to future revenue streams.

Looking ahead, Worksport projects substantial revenue growth from its U.S. tonneau cover sales, with additional contributions from new product launches. The company remains focused on achieving profitability and cash flow positivity in 2026, supported by its scalable U.S. manufacturing strategy and expanding market presence.

Armlogi Holding Corp. is a third-party logistics company based in the United States, providing multi-modal transportation and logistics services, including warehousing, materials management, and customs brokerage. In its latest earnings report for the quarter ended September 30, 2025, Armlogi Holding Corp. reported a net loss of $6.5 million, compared to a loss of $4.6 million in the same period last year. The company generated revenue of $49.5 million, up from $42.5 million in the previous year, but faced increased costs of services totaling $52 million, leading to a gross loss of $2.5 million. Key financial metrics showed a decrease in cash and cash equivalents to $6.5 million from $9.2 million at the end of June 2025, and a reduction in total assets to $156.3 million from $170.2 million. The company’s liabilities also decreased to $134 million from $145.1 million. Despite the losses, Armlogi Holding Corp. is actively working on expanding its service offerings and optimizing operations to improve profitability. The management remains focused on securing additional financing to support ongoing operations and is optimistic about future growth opportunities.

data.title: Alumis Inc. Advances Pipeline Amid Financial Challenges
data.description:
Alumis Inc. ( (ALMS) ) has released its Q3 earnings. Here is a breakdown of the information Alumis Inc. presented to its investors.

Meet Your ETF AI Analyst

  • Discover how TipRanks' ETF AI Analyst can help you make smarter investment decisions
  • Explore ETFs TipRanks' users love and see what insights the ETF AI Analyst reveals about the ones you follow.

Alumis Inc., a clinical-stage biopharmaceutical company, is focused on developing next-generation targeted therapies for immune-mediated diseases. The company is advancing its pipeline with significant progress in its clinical trials, particularly for its oral TYK2 inhibitors aimed at treating conditions like plaque psoriasis and systemic lupus erythematosus.

In its third-quarter 2025 financial report, Alumis highlighted key milestones, including the publication of positive Phase 2 trial results for envudeucitinib in a leading dermatology journal and the promising preclinical data for A-005, a CNS-penetrant TYK2 inhibitor. The company also showcased lonigutamab’s potential in treating thyroid eye disease, with encouraging safety and efficacy outcomes from early-stage studies.

Financially, Alumis reported a net loss of $110.8 million for the quarter, an increase from the previous year, driven by higher research and development expenses and costs related to its merger with ACELYRIN. Despite these losses, the company maintains a strong cash position with $377.7 million in cash, cash equivalents, and marketable securities, which is expected to support its operations into 2027.

Looking ahead, Alumis is poised for significant developments with anticipated data readouts from its ongoing clinical trials in 2026. The company remains committed to leveraging its precision immunology platform to transform treatment options for patients with immune-mediated diseases.

Sable Offshore Corp. is an independent oil and gas company based in Houston, Texas, specializing in offshore oil and gas production. The company recently released its earnings report for the quarter ending September 30, 2025, highlighting significant financial challenges and strategic developments. Despite restarting production at its Santa Ynez field in May 2025, Sable Offshore Corp. reported a net loss of $110.4 million for the quarter, with total operating expenses reaching $119.4 million. The company has been actively pursuing alternative strategies to address regulatory delays and improve its financial position, including an offshore storage and treating vessel strategy to access domestic and global markets. Looking ahead, Sable Offshore Corp. remains focused on securing additional financing and regulatory approvals to continue its operations and achieve first sales from its production assets.

GameSquare Holdings, Inc. is a media, entertainment, and technology company that connects brands and publishers with Gen Z, Gen Alpha, and Millennial audiences through its comprehensive gaming media network.

In its third-quarter earnings report for 2025, GameSquare Holdings announced a net income of $5.9 million from continuing operations, marking a significant turnaround from the previous year’s loss. The company also reported a gross margin increase to 49.4% and highlighted its strong balance sheet with $81.5 million in digital asset treasury (DAT) assets and cash, with no debt.

The acquisition of Click Management has been a strategic move, enhancing GameSquare’s reach into creator-led brand partnerships and accelerating growth in its media and agency ecosystems. The company also launched a digital asset treasury strategy, which has contributed to its financial strength and enabled a share repurchase program.

Looking ahead, GameSquare’s management is optimistic about continued positive momentum in the fourth quarter of 2025, supported by its strategic initiatives and a scalable platform poised for growth in media, creators, gaming, and Web3 sectors.

Evolv Technologies Holdings, Inc. (NASDAQ: EVLV) is a security technology company that specializes in AI-powered screening and analytics solutions for enhancing safety in various public and private venues. The company has recently released its third-quarter financial results for 2025, showcasing significant growth in revenue and improvements in profitability metrics.

For the third quarter of 2025, Evolv Technology reported a revenue of $42.9 million, marking a 57% increase compared to the same period in 2024. The company also achieved an annual recurring revenue of $117.2 million, up by 25% year-over-year. Despite a net loss of $1.8 million, Evolv’s adjusted EBITDA reached $5.1 million, reflecting a positive adjusted EBITDA margin of 12%. The revenue growth was primarily driven by new customer acquisitions and expanded deployments with existing clients.

The company’s financial performance for the first nine months of 2025 also showed a robust 44% increase in total revenue, amounting to $107.4 million. Evolv’s strategic focus on operational efficiency has resulted in a reduction of net loss and improved adjusted earnings compared to the previous year. The company has maintained a strong cash position with $56.2 million in cash, cash equivalents, and marketable securities as of September 30, 2025.

Looking ahead, Evolv Technologies has raised its revenue outlook for 2025 to between $142 million and $145 million, indicating a growth rate of 37% to 40% year-over-year. The management is optimistic about achieving positive full-year adjusted EBITDA and expects to be cash flow positive by the fourth quarter of 2025, driven by continued demand for its innovative security solutions.

data.title: GCI Liberty Reports Q3 2025 Financial Results
data.description:

Meet Your ETF AI Analyst

  • Discover how TipRanks' ETF AI Analyst can help you make smarter investment decisions
  • Explore ETFs TipRanks' users love and see what insights the ETF AI Analyst reveals about the ones you follow.

GCI Liberty, Inc. is a company that operates primarily in the telecommunications sector, providing data, mobile, voice, and managed services to consumer, business, government, and carrier customers throughout Alaska. In its third quarter of 2025 financial results, GCI Liberty reported a 2% decline in revenue to $257 million and an operating loss of $488 million, largely due to a significant non-cash impairment charge. The company also noted a decrease in Adjusted OIBDA by 8% to $92 million. Despite these challenges, GCI Liberty generated $357 million in net cash from operating activities over the trailing twelve months and reported a free cash flow of $155 million. Key financial metrics highlighted a 4% decrease in consumer revenue, driven by declines in video and data services, while wireless services saw growth. Business revenue remained flat, with data service upgrades offsetting declines in wireless revenue. The company announced a $300 million rights offering and emphasized its commitment to expanding broadband connectivity in Alaska, supported by provisional awards of over $140 million in grants. Looking ahead, GCI Liberty remains focused on enhancing its broadband and wireless services, with planned capital expenditures between $225 million and $250 million for the year. The company aims to leverage its financial strategies to support future opportunities and maintain its commitment to closing the digital divide in Alaska.

WaterBridge Infrastructure LLC is a Delaware-based company specializing in the management of water resources for the oil and gas industry, primarily focusing on produced water handling and related services in the Delaware Basin. The company operates in the energy sector, providing essential infrastructure for oil and gas exploration and production activities.

In its latest earnings report for the quarter ending September 30, 2025, WaterBridge Infrastructure LLC highlighted significant growth in its financial performance, driven by increased revenues from produced water handling and water solutions services. The company also completed its initial public offering (IPO) in September 2025, which has bolstered its financial position.

Key financial metrics from the report include a total revenue increase to $123.25 million for the quarter, up from $89.80 million in the same period last year. The company reported a net income of $4.76 million, a substantial improvement from the previous year’s $3.31 million. Additionally, WaterBridge’s IPO raised approximately $672.8 million, which has been used to repay debt and fund future growth projects.

The company’s strategic focus on long-term oil and gas production rather than cyclical drilling activities has positioned it well in the market. WaterBridge’s assets, including water handling facilities and pipelines, are primarily located in key basins such as the Delaware and Eagle Ford, supporting its robust revenue streams.

Looking ahead, WaterBridge Infrastructure LLC’s management remains optimistic about its growth prospects, supported by its strengthened financial position post-IPO and ongoing demand for its services in the energy sector. The company plans to continue expanding its infrastructure and service offerings to capitalize on market opportunities.

data.title: North American Construction Group Reports Mixed Q3 2025 Results
data.description:
North American Construction ( (NOA) ) has released its Q3 earnings. Here is a breakdown of the information North American Construction presented to its investors.

Meet Your ETF AI Analyst

  • Discover how TipRanks' ETF AI Analyst can help you make smarter investment decisions
  • Explore ETFs TipRanks' users love and see what insights the ETF AI Analyst reveals about the ones you follow.

North American Construction Group Ltd. is a leading provider of heavy civil construction and mining services across Australia, Canada, and the U.S., known for its extensive experience in the mining, resource, and infrastructure construction sectors.

In its third quarter earnings report for 2025, North American Construction Group Ltd. reported a 6% increase in combined revenue, reaching $390.8 million, despite facing a 23% decline in gross profit. The company also declared a quarterly dividend of twelve Canadian cents per common share, showcasing its commitment to returning value to shareholders.

Key financial metrics highlighted a mixed performance, with adjusted EPS dropping 44% to $0.67, while net income rose by 19% to $17.3 million. The company’s adjusted EBITDA decreased by 12% to $99.0 million, yet free cash flow saw a significant improvement, increasing by $56.3 million to $45.7 million. The Heavy Equipment – Australia segment was a standout performer, with a 26% increase in revenue driven by fleet expansion and favorable weather conditions.

Operationally, the company benefited from strong execution in Australia, which led to a 4.5% improvement in gross profit margins. In Canada, the gross margin improved by 4.8% due to steady operations, despite a decline in revenue from reduced activity in the oil sands. The company’s joint ventures and affiliates saw an 8% decrease in revenue, primarily due to decreased volumes from the Nuna Group of Companies.

Looking ahead, North American Construction Group Ltd. remains focused on delivering its commitments for the second half of the year, with management expressing confidence in finishing 2025 on a strong note. The company continues to target a net debt leverage of 2.1x, maintaining a stable financial position as it navigates the evolving market landscape.

Clene’s financial position showed a decrease in cash and cash equivalents to $7.9 million as of September 30, 2025, down from $12.2 million at the end of 2024. The company also reported a significant stockholders’ deficit, which increased to $12.4 million by the end of the third quarter. Looking ahead, Clene Inc. faces substantial challenges in securing additional financing to continue its operations and advance its drug development programs. The management has implemented cost-saving measures and is exploring various funding options, but the company’s future remains uncertain as it seeks to overcome its financial hurdles.

Zura Bio Limited is a clinical-stage immunology company focused on developing novel dual-pathway antibodies for autoimmune and inflammatory diseases, with its primary operations in the biotechnology sector.

In its latest earnings report, Zura Bio Limited highlighted its ongoing efforts in advancing its clinical-stage product candidate, tibulizumab, through Phase 2 trials for conditions such as hidradenitis suppurativa and systemic sclerosis, while also exploring additional therapeutic indications for other products in its pipeline.

The company reported a net loss of $18.0 million for the third quarter of 2025, with total operating expenses reaching $19.5 million. Despite the losses, Zura Bio maintains a strong liquidity position with $139.0 million in cash and cash equivalents, which it expects to support its operations over the next twelve months. The company continues to invest heavily in research and development, which accounted for a significant portion of its expenses.

Looking ahead, Zura Bio’s management remains focused on advancing its clinical trials and exploring new development opportunities within its pipeline, aiming to address unmet medical needs in the immunology space. The company is optimistic about its strategic direction and the potential of its product candidates to achieve broader clinical benefits for patients.
